title: Path of Exile
notes: No DX11 support in Steam's config, will crash on first run, then ask to relaunch as DX9. Game plays well. Will eventually crash due to suspected memory leak during an area change. Most susceptible to this crash after around an hour of normal play.

title: Path of Exile
notes: No DX11 support in Steam's config, will crash on first run, then ask to relaunch as DX9.  PoE is otherwise known to run w/DX11, so how do we enable that in Steam?

title: Path of Exile
notes: Works in dx9 mode. Good performance (average about 60fps, highest ~90s and some spikes) on lowest settings. Occasional lag/fps spikes when exploding packs and such, but that's normal for PoE (may also be because of my internet). Levelling experience should be perfectly smooth, but I'd stay away from higher level maps and such unless you have better hardware than me lol

title: Path of Exile
notes: Occasional lag spikes / fps drops

title: Path of Exile
notes: Won't launch.  Just instantly crashes out with nothing displayed.  Tried several drivers.

title: Path of Exile
notes: Game appears to work fine on first glance, but there is an issue that causes a sub 1 second delay with no FPS updates. Seems to be when its trying to handle a new pack of mobs appearing - note its not when the mobs are near the player, just when they are in range to appear so they aren't even graphically displayed yet. Could be the engine already is preparing to render them. Same machine has 0 issues on Windows with PoE. Note this machine gets 55-60 FPS with this in Linux, but these freezes make it unplayable/painful.

title: Path of Exile

Runs OK under DirectX 9 with occasional FPS drops and inferior performance compared to native gameplay.

title: Path of Exile

notes:
Game performance now comparable to Dx9 version of Windows - except one show stopper. The game crashes after time due to a memory failure, that appears to be graphics card memory. Is likely a Dx9 compatibility issue. Dx11 version still crashes on start.
Until the crash is fixed the game can't really be played beyond the first act.

gpu: X.Org AMD Radeon (TM) RX 470 Graphics (POLARIS10, DRM 3.23.0, 4.15.0-34-generic, LLVM 8.0.0)
notes: Crashes on DX11 mode, works only in DX9. The game was very stuttery at first, apparently due to shaders being compiled, especially in the starting town as people with various particle-effect-laden gear were coming in and out.  I crashed four times during 45 minutes of play, twice due to some .dds file being unable to load and other two due to a "cannot create thread" error. The MTX shop's front page also has some black boxes that I assume should have some sort of adverts in them.

title: Path of Exile

notes:
Tried adding "--waitforpreload --nodx9ex --nologo --gc 2" to the launch options. Game loaded fine (DX9 mode onyl) and was able to play without stuttering. However I attempted to go into a new area thats part of the new expansion and it failed to load.

I would say that if you're unable to get into all areas of the game, it's crucially unplayable therefore i'm rating it as Borked.

notes:

notes: Runs pretty much flawlessly with the exception of the stuttering.  I tried the tweaks and it really doesn't change anything.  The stuttering doesn't make it unplayable.  Other then that it runs perfectly fine
title: Path of Exile

notes:
Launcher options PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%, is slow than windows version, as many games it has problems in fullscreen (even in windows has some), i recommend to put this game in windowed borderless to avoid any problem, there are some missing textures freezes when loads new attacks or mobs

Suggested ingame configuration: Window borderless, disable shadows, and depends of your system quality downgrade graphic settings, at the moment i dont have any crashes at all with this configuration

* Screen tearing can be solved from nvidia settings "Force composition pipeline"

There is a bit of stuttering, but very playable.

title: Path of Exile

notes:
Because of some bug after an Ubuntu update, I had to downgrade from the Nvidia 410 to the 396 driver. I tried many kernels to get 410 and 415 to work, but for now, I'll just have to wait for a patch. The 396 is definitely less stable.

I've played many more hours, and on the 396, there are often crashes at important areas. The labyrinth will often glitch and crash--meaning you have to start the labyrinth all over again. One symptom is that the text for maps, monsters, and items begins to drop out letters until it just crashes w/o a popup error. I'll have to play from the command line to see if anything comes up. At other times, there is no issue with the text, but the game crashes with a Windows-looking error box about bad allocation. Also, I've had issues where after dying in the labyrinth, the game doesn't crash, but it just hangs.

So yeah, bronze for nvidia 396 at least.
title: Path of Exile

notes: the game runs a little better than with playonlinux, but since the latest patches it often crashes even before you reach the city after login. After several tries you may end in the city, but it crashes when you change the zone, maximu possible play time is 2 - 8 minutes. PoE has always had trouble running in Linux since it keeps using more and more memory, but since about half a year it has been more or less unplayable.
title: Path of Exile

notes: Platinum only for DX9 EX renderer (in options menu).  Works perfectly except for some stuttering when shadows are enabled.  Crashed one time in 12 hours while playing around with graphics options.  DX11 does not work.
title: Path of Exile

notes:
I played from the beginning until around the end of act 3, and that all seemed playable. I didn't find anything that would outright not work.

The issue is that the game will frequently crash, and there doesn't seem to be any rhyme nor reason to it. All I can say is it seems loading more areas accelerate the crashing, and you can kinda tell when it's about to happen based on how much text, or how many textures get corrupted (it's like watching an int overflow on the NES)

performance was steady 60FPS aside from loading new areas, and a dash ability that seemed to cause a lag spike. Nothing to complain about really. I had the game running in DX9 mode most of the time as DX11 wouldn't work, and DX9ex apparently is more buggy than DX9 (I couldn't really tell a major difference when I was testing)

additionally I ran with these arguments, they seem to make the game crash somewhat less frequently.
--waitforpreload --nologo --nodx9ex --gc2
title: Path of Exile

notes: DX9 EX, disabled shadows. Ran flawlessly for several hours.
title: Path of Exile

notes: Is generally playable with stuttering, occasional crashes with "bad allocation" errors.
title: Path of Exile

notes: I combine all different flags with internal configuration, maybe you can play for 3 hours with no issue, but suddenly when you pass thought a portal or charging a new level the game crashes, after hours of testing with lutris version that uses tgk patched wine with no issue at all,  i grab command line options used in lutris to steam-proton, the first is must have (preloads textures, shaders): --waitforpreload --nologo --nodx9ex --gc2 but with proton still crashing randomly :(
title: Path of Exile

notes:
Only runs in DX9, run in normal DX9 and not EX. Also disable Shadows for smoother performance.

Game has following issues. 
Random stuttering, mostly minor but can be quite a bit especially during boss fights.
Occasional minor missing textures and models, some pop back in, some don't.
Crashes semi-often, mostly on area changes.

All above issues don't seem to have any significant pattern as to why and crash errors are random. Game is definitely playable however.

notes:
Game boots up, can actually play the game, but when it comes to changing zones - there is like a 75% chance the game will crash. A couple restarts will get you a little progress, but crashing 4-8 times an hour is really... really... not that enjoyable. 

Not sure why people are putting PLAT/GOLD/SILVER, game crashes more often than not preventing you from playing comfortably and getting cozy.

notes: Crashes on changing Levels

notes: Game runs fine the longer you play it thanks to the shader cache. Initial combat usually results in stutters caused by shader compilation, which gets better over time, as mentioned before. In my session, I didn't get any game crash, but it shouldn't be considered relevant in my case due to my playtime.

title: Path of Exile

notes: menus do not display text, PROTON_NO_D3D11 hangs much less
title: Path of Exile

notes: DirectX11 does not launch. DirectX9 or DirectX9EX launch the game but the game is almost guaranteed to crash with an area transition. I have lowered graphic settings but this appears to have no effect. I have updated my launch options based on the various advice to "PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% --waitforpreload --noasync --nologo --nodx9ex --gc2". Adding the Large Address Aware seems to have decreased the crashes but I can't confirm whether this is the actual setting that improves gameplay or if it is temporary. Disabling mutlithreading decreased crashes but this was only temporary. Crashes increased the next gameplay. Generally unplayable.
title: Path of Exile

notes: It runs okay on the lowest settings. But it takes a long time to load textures. If you go an area with a lot of textures it has to load at once it will crash. Even while going slowly eventually you'll just crash. Longest time I've managed to go without a crash is like 45 minutes.
title: Path of Exile

notes: Initially, launching the game caused it to immediately crash and offer to start it in DirectX9 or DirectX9EX, instead of DirectX11. Accepting to start in DirectX9EX started the game, but it has a lot of stuttering and lag spikes seemingly whenever new content occurs, such as a new enemy or a new spell appearing. Plus, it would commonly crash whenever entering a new zone. Using the launch options --waitforpreload and --noasync helped, but the lag was still there and the game still crashed.
